nl: Pretend MBSE Requirements — Portable Variable Soft-Toss Training Machine :root{ --bg:#0b1020; --card:#121b33; --text:#eaf0ff; --muted:#b8c2e6; --border:#25345e; --accent:#7aa7ff; } *{box-sizing:border-box} body{ margin:0; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, "Apple Color Emoji","Segoe UI Emoji"; background: radial-gradient(1100px 700px at 20% 0%, #162048 0%, var(--bg) 62%); color:var(--text); line-height:1.55; } header{ border-bottom:1px solid var(--border); padding:26px 16px 14px; } .wrap{ max-width: 980px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 16px 44px; } h1{margin:0 0 6px;font-size:1.55rem;letter-spacing:.2px} .subtitle{margin:0;color:var(--muted);font-size:.98rem} section{ background: rgba(18,27,51,.78); border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:14px; margin:14px 0; padding:16px 16px 10px; box-shadow: 0 14px 30px rgba(0,0,0,.25); } h2{margin:0 0 10px;font-size:1.15rem;color:#f4f7ff} .req{ padding:10px 12px; margin:10px 0; border-radius:12px; border:1px solid var(--border); background: rgba(11,16,32,.55); } .req strong{display:block;margin-bottom:4px;color:#fff} .req p{margin:0;color:var(--text)} .note{margin:6px 0 0;color:var(--muted);font-size:.92rem} ul,ol{margin:10px 0 6px 18px} li{margin:6px 0} hr{border:0;border-top:1px solid var(--border);margin:12px 0 0} footer{margin-top:16px;color:var(--muted);font-size:.9rem;padding: 6px 2px 0} Pretend MBSE Requirements — Portable Variable Soft-Toss Training Machine Solution-agnostic requirements and constraints intended to drive MBSE functional decomposition and concept exploration for a softball/baseball soft-toss training machine that provides varied tosses rather than a single repeated toss. 1) Mission and scope requirements R-MSN-001 (Primary objective) The system shall deliver a soft-toss ball to a batter to support batting practice. R-MSN-002 (Practice realism) The system shall provide a non-repetitive practice experience by varying toss outcomes rather than repeating a single fixed toss. R-MSN-003 (Stakeholders) The system shall support use by players and coaches during practice sessions. R-MSN-004 (Use environments) The system shall be operable in typical practice environments including grass/dirt fields, gymnasiums, and backyards. 2) Top-level functional requirements R-FUNC-001 (Ball delivery) The system shall deliver a regulation softball (or equivalent practice ball) from a machine output location toward a batter’s hitting area. R-FUNC-002 (Varied toss parameters) The system shall be capable of varying toss height , speed , and direction/angle across successive tosses. R-FUNC-003 (Toss location variability) The system shall be capable of delivering tosses to different locations within a user-defined target region appropriate for batting drills. R-FUNC-004 (Timing control) The system shall provide a user-defined toss interval (time between tosses), including the ability to adjust the interval during practice. R-FUNC-005 (Consistency mode) The system shall support a repeatable mode where, for a given configuration, toss outcomes remain consistent within a defined tolerance for training drills. R-FUNC-006 (Misfire prevention) The system shall prevent or detect common feed/launch faults (e.g., no-toss events, weak tosses, or multiple balls entering the launch path at once) and shall respond in a safe, predictable manner. 3) Performance requirements (measurable targets) R-PERF-001 (Targeting capability) The system shall be able to intentionally direct tosses to selected portions of a defined strike-zone-like region after a calibration/setup procedure. R-PERF-002 (Repeatability under controlled feed) When operated with a consistent ball feed condition, the system shall achieve shot-to-shot landing-location repeatability within a small tolerance band suitable for batting drills. R-PERF-003 (Useful operating distance) The system shall support an operating distance range suitable for soft-toss drills while maintaining practical accuracy in that range. R-PERF-004 (Cycle rate) The system shall support repeated toss cycles over a full practice session without performance degradation that prevents normal use. 4) Key constraints R-CNST-001 (Portability) The system shall be portable and lightweight enough for a single person to transport and set up for practice. R-CNST-002 (Environmental robustness) The system shall tolerate dust/dirt exposure and outdoor weather variability typical of practice environments. R-CNST-003 (Protective enclosure) The system shall include protective measures to shield internal components from field conditions and weather exposure. R-CNST-004 (Cost target) The system shall be designed to achieve a target end-user price range of $150–$300 . R-CNST-005 (Manufacturability) The system shall be compatible with a simple, low-cost manufacturing approach suitable for repeat production. R-CNST-006 (Service life) The system shall be designed for a long service life under repeated use and typical handling/transport conditions. 5) Interfaces and usability requirements R-INT-001 (User controls) The system shall provide user controls for: (a) enabling/disabling operation, (b) setting toss timing, and (c) selecting or enabling variation of toss outcomes. R-INT-002 (Ease of use) The system shall be easy to set up and operate by a coach or player with minimal training. R-INT-003 (User feedback) The system shall provide basic operational feedback (e.g., ready, active, fault/stop) that is understandable in a practice environment. R-INT-004 (Power source) The system shall support a reliable power source appropriate for field and indoor use and shall protect users from electrical hazards. R-INT-005 (Transport and storage) The system shall support convenient transport and storage (e.g., compact form, manageable handling features) without requiring disassembly beyond normal stow steps. 6) Safety and assurance requirements R-SAFE-001 (Emergency stop) The system shall provide an emergency stop function that immediately transitions the system to a safe state. R-SAFE-002 (Guarding) The system shall prevent user contact with hazardous moving elements during normal operation and typical setup/maintenance actions. R-SAFE-003 (Safe fault response) In the presence of detected faults (e.g., jam, abnormal operation, control error), the system shall enter a safe state that prevents unintended ball launch or unsafe motion. R-SAFE-004 (Impact resistance) The system shall tolerate incidental impacts expected in a practice environment (e.g., stray ball contact) without creating unsafe conditions. 7) Reliability, maintainability, and durability requirements R-RAM-001 (Practice-session reliability) The system shall operate reliably for an entire practice session without requiring frequent user intervention to restore normal function. R-RAM-002 (Maintenance accessibility) The system shall allow routine maintenance tasks (cleaning, inspection, simple adjustments) using common tools and without specialized equipment. R-RAM-003 (Dust/weather considerations) The design shall explicitly address environmental failure risks (dirt, weather exposure) through protective design and maintainable interfaces. R-RAM-004 (Wear and deformation control) The design shall control wear and deformation of high-use components such that performance remains within specified tolerances over the intended service life. 8) Verification-oriented requirements R-VER-001 (Accuracy verification) The system shall be verifiable by a test demonstrating the ability to deliver balls to selected target sub-regions after setup/calibration. R-VER-002 (Repeatability verification) The system shall be verifiable by a repeatability test that measures shot-to-shot variability under a controlled feed condition. R-VER-003 (Variation verification) The system shall be verifiable by demonstrating measurable variation in toss height, speed, and direction across a defined sequence. R-VER-004 (Environmental verification) The system shall be verifiable by an exposure/ingress test representative of dust/dirt and light weather conditions expected during typical practice use. R-VER-005 (Portability verification) The system shall be verifiable by demonstrating transport, setup, and stow operations by a single person using only integrated handling features. Logical system breakdown (requirements-oriented functional “boxes”) Ball Handling & Storage Subsystem Store practice balls for repeated cycles Present a single ball per cycle to the feed interface Prevent double-feeds and support clearing of jams Ball Feed Subsystem Transfer one ball per cycle into the delivery/launch path Maintain consistent feed conditions across operating configurations Support adjustable cycle timing / feed cadence Ball Delivery Subsystem Impart controlled motion to the ball to achieve a soft-toss trajectory Support adjustable toss speed and usable range Support consistent outcomes for a fixed configuration Aiming / Direction Control Subsystem Adjust toss direction to deliver balls to different target locations Support variation across tosses without excessive setup burden Provide a stable, repeatable aiming reference after setup Timing & Control Subsystem Coordinate feed, delivery, and aiming functions per cycle Provide user-selectable toss interval and variation settings Detect faults and command safe-state transitions Power & Electrical Protection Subsystem Provide reliable operational power in indoor/outdoor practice contexts Provide electrical protection appropriate for user-facing equipment Support safe power-up, power-down, and emergency stop behavior Structure, Enclosure & Environmental Protection Subsystem Provide a stable base and alignment for all functional subsystems Protect internal components from dust/dirt and weather exposure Support portability, handling, and storage constraints User Interface & Safety Subsystem Provide intuitive controls and operational feedback Prevent user contact with hazards during operation Provide emergency stop and safe fault response behavior End of requirements set. (No solution concepts included.) Generated as a pretend MBSE requirements artifact to drive functional decomposition and concept exploration; not a final product specification.
